perturbation.original_text: 1. Confidential Information. "Confidential Information" shall mean and include any information which relates to the financial and/or business operations of each party, including, but not limited to, information relating to each party's customers, products, processes, financial condition, employees, manufacturing techniques, experimental work and/or trade secrets. Said Confidential Information is deemed proprietary by the parties hereto. The Subject Matter of this Agreement, as well as any additional information disclosed hereunder that is deemed Confidential Information by the parties is described in Exhibit A attached hereto and incorporated herein by this reference.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Confidential Information. "Confidential Information" shall mean and include any information which relates to the financial and/or business operations of each party, including, but not limited to, information relating to each party's customers, products, processes, financial condition, employees, manufacturing techniques, experimental work and/or trade secrets. Said Confidential Information is deemed proprietary by the parties hereto. The Subject Matter of this Agreement, as well as any additional information disclosed hereunder is described in Exhibit A attached hereto and incorporated herein by this reference.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'that is deemed Confidential Information by the parties' was removed. This creates an in-text contradiction because while Exhibit A is incorporated, it no longer explicitly defines the information disclosed there as 'Confidential Information'. Section 2 mandates specific usage restrictions only for 'Confidential Information,' leading to uncertainty on whether the Exhibit A information falls under these restrictions, contradicting the intent of the original definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information. Each party agrees not to use the other's Confidential Information for any purpose other than for the specific consultation regarding the subject matter of this Agreement. Any other use of such Confidential Information shall be made only upon the prior written consent from an authorized representative of the party which disclosed such information (hereinafter the "Disclosing Party") or pursuant to subsequent agreement between the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Subject Matter of the Agreement, as detailed in Exhibit A, is 'deemed Confidential Information by the parties'. The changed text omits the phrase 'that is deemed Confidential Information by the parties'. This omission creates a contradiction because Section 2 mandates specific usage restrictions only for 'Confidential Information.' Without the explicit statement that Exhibit A defines 'Confidential Information', it becomes uncertain whether the information in Exhibit A falls under these restrictions, which directly contradicts the intent and enforceability of Section 2.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that from the date of receipt, the party receiving the Confidential Information (hereinafter the "Receiving Party") shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity or use it for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not publish, divulge, communicate, or reveal any Confidential Information to any person, corporation, or other third party or to any of Receiving Party's employees who do not have a need to know such Confidential Information with respect to their job duties. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it would with respect to its own confidential information. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement. "Confidential Information" shall not include information, technical data or know-how which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure and is not otherwise subject to restriction; (b) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party who has no obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party; or (e) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that from the date of receipt, the party receiving the Confidential Information (hereinafter the "Receiving Party") shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity or use it for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not publish, divulge, communicate, or reveal any Confidential Information to any person, corporation, or other third party or to any of Receiving Party's employees who do not have a need to know such Confidential Information with respect to their job duties. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it would with respect to its own confidential information. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement. "Confidential Information" shall not include information, technical data or know-how which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure and is not otherwise subject to restriction; (b) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party who has no obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; or (e) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Clause (d) 'is independently developed by the Receiving Party;' was removed. This causes an in-text contradiction because without this clause, any information independently developed, even if similar to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, is technically subject to the non-disclosure agreement according to the first part of Section 3, which states 'The Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information'. This restricts the Receiving Party's ability to use its own independently developed knowledge, contradicting implied rights to use its own innovations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that from the date of receipt, the party receiving the Confidential Information (hereinafter the "Receiving Party") shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity or use it for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not publish, divulge, communicate, or reveal any Confidential Information to any person, corporation, or other third party or to any of Receiving Party's employees who do not have a need to know such Confidential Information with respect to their job duties. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it would with respect to its own confidential information. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement. "Confidential Information" shall not include information, technical data or know-how which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure and is not otherwise subject to restriction; (b) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party who has no obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; or (e) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes clause (d) from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. Clause (d) stated that information independently developed by the Receiving Party was not considered confidential. The contradicted text, which is the same as the original, states that the Receiving Party cannot disclose 'Confidential Information'. By removing clause (d), the changed text now implies that independently developed information could be considered 'Confidential Information', which directly contradicts the Receiving Party's ability to use its own innovations, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information and copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to Disclosing Party three years from the effective date of this Agreement, or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the Disclosing Party for the return of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Information. All Information and copies and extracts thereof shall be promptly returned to Disclosing Party three years from the effective date of this Agreement, or at any time within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request by the Disclosing Party for the return of such Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Information', the clause now applies to all information, not just confidential. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 3 defines specific exceptions to what is considered Confidential Information. Now, all information, even that explicitly excluded from confidentiality under Section 3, must be returned, contradicting the explicit exclusions in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that from the date of receipt, the party receiving the Confidential Information (hereinafter the "Receiving Party") shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity or use it for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not publish, divulge, communicate, or reveal any Confidential Information to any person, corporation, or other third party or to any of Receiving Party's employees who do not have a need to know such Confidential Information with respect to their job duties. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it would with respect to its own confidential information. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement. "Confidential Information" shall not include information, technical data or know-how which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure and is not otherwise subject to restriction; (b) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party who has no obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; or (e) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Information' broadens the scope of Section 5 to include all information, not just confidential information. This contradicts Section 3, which specifically excludes certain types of information from being considered 'Confidential Information'. Therefore, requiring the return of *all* information directly contradicts the exclusions defined in Section 3, creating a strong in-text contradiction. Section 3 defines certain exceptions to 'Confidential Information' such as information already known, public information or information disclosed by a third party. The changed text would require this information to be returned even though it is explictly not considered confidential, leading to a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Arbitration and Equitable Relief. (a) Arbitration. Except as provided in Section 8(b) below, the parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled by arbitration to be held in San Diego County, California in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. In the event of arbitration, a reasonable amount of discovery may be undertaken by the parties. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final, conclusive and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court having jurisdiction. (b) Equitable Remedies. The parties agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate the Disclosing Party's damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein. Accordingly, the parties agree that if in the event of a breach of any of the covenants contained in this Agreement, the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement. The parties further agree that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and the parties hereby consent to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance. (c) Legal Expenses. If any action or proceeding is brought for the enforcement of this Agreement, or because of an alleged or actual dispute, breach, default, or misrepresentation in connection with any of the provisions of this Agreement, the successful or prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and other costs incurred in such action or proceeding in addition to any other relief to which it may be entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Dispute Resolution. (a) General. Except as provided in Section 8(b) below, the parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled in a manner agreed upon by both parties. (b) Equitable Remedies. The parties agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate the Disclosing Party's damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein. Accordingly, the parties agree that if in the event of a breach of any of the covenants contained in this Agreement, the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement. The parties further agree that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and the parties hereby consent to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance. (c) Legal Expenses. If any action or proceeding is brought for the enforcement of this Agreement, or because of an alleged or actual dispute, breach, default, or misrepresentation in connection with any of the provisions of this Agreement, the successful or prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and other costs incurred in such action or proceeding in addition to any other relief to which it may be entitled.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 8(a) described mandatory arbitration. By changing the title to 'Dispute Resolution' and the text to 'settled in a manner agreed upon by both parties', the clause now suggests mutual agreement on dispute resolution methods. This contradicts the clause 8(b) Equitable Remedies, which still retains the verbiage about court availment and the parties hereby consent to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance. This creates an in-text contradiction, because if parties are to decide the manner of dispute, resorting to a court of law doesn't imply agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Dispute Resolution. (a) General. Except as provided in Section 8(b) below, the parties agree that any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance or breach of this Agreement shall be settled in a manner agreed upon by both parties. (b) Equitable Remedies. The parties agree that it would be impossible or inadequate to measure and calculate the Disclosing Party's damages from any breach of the covenants set forth herein. Accordingly, the parties agree that if in the event of a breach of any of the covenants contained in this Agreement, the affected party will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement. The parties further agree that no bond or other security shall be required in obtaining such equitable relief and the parties hereby consent to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance. (c) Legal Expenses. If any action or proceeding is brought for the enforcement of this Agreement, or because of an alleged or actual dispute, breach, default, or misrepresentation in connection with any of the provisions of this Agreement, the successful or prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and other costs incurred in such action or proceeding in addition to any other relief to which it may be entitled.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated arbitration. The changed text removes this mandate, stating that disputes will be resolved 'in a manner agreed upon by both parties.' However, section 8(b) Equitable Remedies still discusses seeking injunctions from a court and the parties consenting to such actions. This creates a direct conflict because resorting to court action directly contradicts the idea of settling disputes via mutual agreement, thus resulting in an in-text contradiction as per the explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Term. This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the Effective Date, but may be terminated prior to expiration by either party giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other party; provided, however, the obligations to protect the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement shall survive for a period of five (5) years from the date of the last disclosure of Confidential Information is made under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Term. This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the Effective Date, but may be terminated prior to expiration by either party giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other party; provided, however, the obligations to protect the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'for a period of five (5) years from the date of the last disclosure of Confidential Information is made under this Agreement.', the survival of confidentiality obligations is now indefinite after termination. This creates an in-text contradiction with the first sentence stating that the agreement expires two years from the Effective Date. If the agreement expires after two years, it is unclear how confidentiality can 'survive the termination of this Agreement' indefinitely, as the termination would signal the end of all obligations, including those related to confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Term. This Agreement shall expire two (2) years from the Effective Date, but may be terminated prior to expiration by either party giving thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the other party; provided, however, the obligations to protect the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific duration (five years) for which confidentiality obligations survive after termination. This omission directly contradicts the original intent of Section 9, which specifies a two-year expiration of the agreement. By making the confidentiality obligations indefinite after termination, it creates a conflict because the agreement's overall expiry after two years implies all obligations, including confidentiality, should cease at that point, creating an in-text contradiction based on the given explanation and directly altering an obligation that causes confusion.

perturbation.original_text: EXHIBIT A SUBJECT MATTER; ADDITIONAL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION HNC (which shall be considered the Disclosing Party for purposes of this Exhibit A) may disclose information with respect to the following in connection with this Agreement. The parties agree that any such information disclosed shall be considered Confidential Information for purposes of this Agreement: Any and all information from any and all verbal, electronic, and/or written communications (whether in the form of slides, handouts, letters, memoranda, agreements, facsimile transmissions, meetings, conference and other telephone calls, diskettes, files, tapes, and/or any other mode) with respect to the HNC products known as SelectCast(TM), Convectis(TM), related products, and/or related concepts, proposals, data sources, plans, markets, customers, pricing, schedules, development efforts (including future product functionality and release plans), decision technology and/or models, software (including source code, object code and/or documentation), numerical data processing algorithms, product and software design specifications and/or functionality, and/or ideas.
perturbation.changed_text: EXHIBIT A SUBJECT MATTER; ADDITIONAL INFORMATION HNC (which shall be considered the Disclosing Party for purposes of this Exhibit A) may disclose information with respect to the following in connection with this Agreement. The parties agree that any such information disclosed shall be considered Public Information for purposes of this Agreement: Any and all information from any and all verbal, electronic, and/or written communications (whether in the form of slides, handouts, letters, memoranda, agreements, facsimile transmissions, meetings, conference and other telephone calls, diskettes, files, tapes, and/or any other mode) with respect to the HNC products known as SelectCast(TM), Convectis(TM), related products, and/or related concepts, proposals, data sources, plans, markets, customers, pricing, schedules, development efforts (including future product functionality and release plans), decision technology and/or models, software (including source code, object code and/or documentation), numerical data processing algorithms, product and software design specifications and/or functionality, and/or ideas.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Public Information' in Exhibit A, it now explicitly states that the listed information is to be considered public. This creates a direct in-text contradiction with Section 3, which outlines restrictions on disclosing 'Confidential Information.' If Exhibit A details 'Public Information', then the restrictions of Section 3 should not apply, effectively nullifying the confidentiality obligations for the information described in Exhibit A despite Section 3 aiming to protect Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Proprietary Information. The parties agree that from the date of receipt, the party receiving the Confidential Information (hereinafter the "Receiving Party") shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity or use it for its own benefit except as provided in this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not publish, divulge, communicate, or reveal any Confidential Information to any person, corporation, or other third party or to any of Receiving Party's employees who do not have a need to know such Confidential Information with respect to their job duties. The Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to avoid publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it would with respect to its own confidential information. These efforts shall specifically include document control measures, such as numbered copies and sign out logs, and imposing on all employees, agents and other representatives of Receiving Party restrictions at least as strict as required by this Agreement. "Confidential Information" shall not include information, technical data or know-how which: (a) is already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure and is not otherwise subject to restriction; (b) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Receiving Party; (c) rightfully disclosed to Receiving Party by a third party who has no obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party; or (e) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Confidential Information' to 'Public Information' within Exhibit A. This directly contradicts Section 3, which outlines the non-disclosure obligations for 'Confidential Information'. By explicitly stating that the information listed in Exhibit A is 'Public Information', it implies that the restrictions of Section 3 should not apply to it, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the confidentiality obligations for that information.
